input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

### 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime
`StaticResource` resolves **once** at load time. For runtime theme switching, use **`DynamicResource`**:

```xml
<!-- ✗ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ Will update -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by merging the new dictionary into `Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries` at runtime.

### 2. Android restarts the Activity on UI-mode change
By default, Android **recreates** the activity when dark mode toggles. Prevent this by adding `UiMode` to `ConfigChanges` in your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    // add UiMode here ↓
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation
        | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← prevents restart
        | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
        | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With `UiMode` declared, Android will call `OnConfigurationChanged` instead of destroying/recreating the activity. You can listen for it and swap your `DynamicResource` dictionaries accordingly.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user toggles dark mode in Android settings, the app restarts (activity is recreated). This is because Android recreates the activity on configuration changes like `uiMode`. They need to handle this in their Android configuration.


I'll walk through the solution for both issues.
